prompt: A screenshot of a real macOS app called ImageView showing the Loupe view. The window has a seamless dark title bar (#0c0c12) with macOS traffic lights (red/yellow/green dots) in the top-left. Next to the traffic lights, monospace tab labels: '1 Grid  2 Compare  3 Loupe  4 Canvas  5 Lineage  6 Embeddings  7 Export'. The '3 Loupe' tab has a thin green #9ece6a underline, others are gray #7a7fa0. On the far right of the tab bar: '160px' text and a minimal slider (thin line with a small dot). NO SIDEBAR in Loupe mode — the image fills the entire width below the tab bar. The main area shows a mobile app screenshot (a task management app with an 'Add task' dialog) displayed centered on pure black #08080c background, object-fit contain style. At the very bottom, a single status bar (#0c0c12, 32px) showing: left side 'LOUPE' in green and filename in gray, right side keyboard hints in dim gray 'h/l:prev/next  f:fullscreen  +/-:zoom  Esc:back'. The overall feel is surgical, dark, monospace — like a premium developer tool. No rounded corners on anything. 1px borders only.
